primary_goal: Build authority for UK ecommerce and wholesale automation through high-quality, specific, operator-led articles. Quality beats quantity; avoid keyword spam and thin SEO pages.
site_structure_note: The public article hub is /articles/. Future posts should use /articles/ unless instructed otherwise.
ai_mention_rule: AI may be covered when it helps the offer: process design, clean data, controls, review rules and ROI proof must come before any tool. Do not frame AI as magic, as a replacement for Henry's work, or as a replacement for the client's judgement.

articles.title: Ecommerce AI automation: how to prove the ROI first
articles.topic_type: Trend-aware commercial advice and operator-led automation ROI guidance
articles.trend_search_signal: Used only where it supports the Olivers Consulting offer: AI is framed as one possible tool inside a controlled workflow, not as the offer itself.
articles.primary_search_intent: A UK ecommerce operator is considering AI automation and wants a practical way to choose a use case, control risk and test the return before buying software or commissioning a build.
articles.authority_value: Connects AI interest to Olivers Consulting's core position: map the process, find the payroll leak, keep human review on exceptions and prove payback with simple maths.
articles.duplicate_check: Distinct from the hiring article, stock-sync article and supplier-file article. It focuses on ROI proof and governance before choosing tools.
articles.hemingway_readability_check: Passed final plain-language editor check for grade 9 or lower.
articles.search_console_submission: Not submitted from this runtime; sitemap updated for later Search Console recrawl.

articles.title: Why stock sync fails between Shopify, Amazon and eBay
articles.topic_type: Commercial SEO and operator-led stock control advice

articles.authority_value: Builds the channel operations pillar with practical rules for source of truth, buffers, bundles, timing gaps and exception handling.
articles.duplicate_check: Distinct from supplier data, reporting and hire-versus-automation articles; covers multi-channel stock control and stock-sync failure modes.

quality_rules: Write for UK ecommerce and wholesale operators first, not search engines.
Quality beats quantity; two posts per week is a ceiling, not a quota.
Every article and supporting copy must read at Hemingway grade 9 or lower.
Use current trend and keyword signals to choose useful buyer questions where available, but never keyword-stuff.
AI may be mentioned when it supports the offer: ROI, controls, review rules, process design and practical payback.
Do not frame AI as a replacement for Henry's work or the client's judgement.
No filler, generic automation hype or repeated points.
No keyword spam or pages that exist only to target a phrase.
Do not invent client results, statistics, dates, prices or case studies.
Every article must include a clear decision point, operational example or ROI calculation where relevant.
After the starter articles, use scheduled publishing or refreshes rather than adding volume for its own sake.
